title: How does the @Autowired annotation work, and how is it used for dependency injection?

answers.junior: @Autowired tells Spring to automatically provide the needed object for a field, constructor, or setter.
answers.middle: @Autowired injects dependencies by type. Use it on fields, constructors, or setters. Constructor injection is preferred for clarity and testability. Use @Qualifier if multiple beans exist.
answers.senior: @Autowired works with component scanning to inject beans. Constructor injection is best for mandatory dependencies. If no bean is found, Spring throws an error unless required=false is set. Avoid field injection for testability.
answers.tricky: @Autowired uses reflection to resolve dependencies. Ambiguity is handled with @Qualifier. Field injection complicates encapsulation and testing. Component scanning discovers beans, and Spring injects them at runtime.
